title: Exallage lapeyrousei
taxonomy: Exallage lapeyrousei is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. This plant is native to Madagascar and is classified under the genus Exallage. Exallage lapeyrousei is part of the larger Rubiaceae family, which contains over 13,000 species of flowering plants. The classification of Exallage lapeyrousei is based on its unique characteristics and genetic makeup. Through its classification, scientists can better understand the relationships between different plant species and the evolution of plant diversity.

intro: Exallage lapeyrousei, also known as Guettarda lapeyrousei, is a medicinal plant species belonging to the family Rubiaceae. This plant has been traditionally used for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in reducing fever, treating wounds, and combating infections. The bioactive constituents of Exallage lapeyrousei include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. Exallage lapeyrousei is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is also found in some traditional medicine remedies, particularly in the French Guiana region.
